description: Fully satisfies all the requirements of the task; clearly presents a fully developed response.
Covers all requirements of the task sufficiently; presents a clear overview of main trends, differences or stages.
Covers the requirements of the task; presents a clear overview of main trends, differences or stages; clearly presents and highlights key features.
Addresses the requirements of the task; presents an overview with information appropriately selected; presents and adequately highlights key features/bullet points.
Generally addresses the task; the format may be inappropriate in places; recounts details mechanically with no clear overview.
Uses cohesion in such a way that it attracts no attention; skilfully manages paragraphing.
Sequences information and ideas logically; manages all aspects of cohesion well; uses paragraphing sufficiently and appropriately.
Logically organises information and ideas; there is clear progression throughout; uses a range of cohesive devices appropriately.
Arranges information and ideas coherently; there is a clear overall progression; uses cohesive devices effectively but may be faulty or mechanical.
Presents information with some organisation but there may be a lack of overall progression; makes inadequate or inaccurate use of cohesive devices.
Uses a wide range of vocabulary with very natural and sophisticated control of lexical features; rare minor errors occur only as slips.
Uses a wide range of vocabulary fluently and flexibly to convey precise meanings; skilfully uses uncommon lexical items with occasional inaccuracies.
Uses a sufficient range of vocabulary to allow some flexibility and precision; uses less common lexical items with some awareness of style and collocation.
Uses an adequate range of vocabulary for the task; attempts to use less common vocabulary but with some inaccuracy; makes some errors in spelling and word formation.
Uses a limited range of vocabulary; may make noticeable errors in spelling and word formation that may cause some difficulty for the reader.
Uses a wide range of structures with full flexibility and accuracy; rare minor errors occur only as slips.
Uses a wide range of structures; the majority of sentences are error-free; makes only very occasional errors or inappropriacies.
Uses a variety of complex structures; produces frequent error-free sentences; has good control of grammar and punctuation but may make a few errors.
Uses a mix of simple and complex sentence forms; makes some errors in grammar and punctuation but they rarely reduce communication.
Uses only a limited range of structures; attempts complex sentences but these tend to be less accurate than simple sentences.
Fully addresses all parts of the task; presents a fully developed position in answer to the question with relevant, fully extended and well supported ideas.
Sufficiently addresses all parts of the task; presents a well-developed response to the question with relevant, extended and supported ideas.
Addresses all parts of the task; presents a clear position throughout the response; presents, extends and supports main ideas, but there may be a tendency to over-generalise.
Addresses all parts of the task although some parts may be more fully covered than others; presents a relevant position although the conclusions may become unclear or repetitive.
Addresses the task only partially; the format may be inappropriate in places; expresses a position but the development is not always clear.
Uses cohesion in such a way that it attracts no attention; skilfully manages paragraphing.
Sequences information and ideas logically; manages all aspects of cohesion well; uses paragraphing sufficiently and appropriately.
Logically organises information and ideas; there is clear progression throughout; uses a range of cohesive devices appropriately although there may be some under-/over-use.
Arranges information and ideas coherently and there is a clear overall progression; uses cohesive devices effectively, but cohesion within and/or between sentences may be faulty or mechanical.
Presents information with some organisation but there may be a lack of overall progression; makes inadequate, inaccurate or over-use of cohesive devices.
Uses a wide range of vocabulary with very natural and sophisticated control of lexical features; rare minor errors occur only as slips.
Uses a wide range of vocabulary fluently and flexibly to convey precise meanings; skilfully uses uncommon lexical items but there may be occasional inaccuracies in word choice and collocation.

Uses a sufficient range of vocabulary to allow some flexibility and precision; uses less common lexical items with some awareness of style and collocation; may produce occasional errors in word choice, spelling and/or word formation.

Uses an adequate range of vocabulary for the task; attempts to use less common vocabulary but with some inaccuracy; makes some errors in spelling and/or word formation, but they do not impede communication.
Uses a limited range of vocabulary, but this is minimally adequate for the task; may make noticeable errors in spelling and/or word formation that may cause some difficulty for the reader.
Uses a wide range of structures with full flexibility and accuracy; rare minor errors occur only as slips.
Uses a wide range of structures; the majority of sentences are error-free; makes only very occasional errors or inappropriacies.
Uses a variety of complex structures; produces frequent error-free sentences; has good control of grammar and punctuation but may make a few errors.
Uses a mix of simple and complex sentence forms; makes some errors in grammar and punctuation but they rarely reduce communication.
Uses only a limited range of structures; attempts complex sentences but these tend to be less accurate than simple sentences; may make frequent grammatical errors.
Fully satisfies all the requirements of the task; clearly presents a fully developed response with appropriate tone throughout.
Fully explains the purpose of the letter; covers all bullet points clearly and consistently; uses a consistently appropriate tone.
Explains the purpose of the letter; covers all bullet points; presents a tone that is appropriate and consistent.
Answers the main points of the task; the purpose is generally clear; may use an inappropriate tone in some parts.
Generally addresses the task; the format may be inappropriate in places; tone may be inconsistent.
Uses cohesion in such a way that it attracts no attention; skilfully manages paragraphing.
Sequences information and ideas logically; manages all aspects of cohesion well; uses paragraphing sufficiently and appropriately.
Logically organises information and ideas; there is clear progression throughout; uses a range of cohesive devices appropriately.
Arranges information and ideas coherently; there is a clear overall progression; uses cohesive devices effectively but may be faulty or mechanical.
Presents information with some organisation but there may be a lack of overall progression; makes inadequate or inaccurate use of cohesive devices.
Uses a wide range of vocabulary with very natural and sophisticated control of lexical features; rare minor errors occur only as slips.
Uses a wide range of vocabulary fluently and flexibly to convey precise meanings; skilfully uses uncommon lexical items with occasional inaccuracies.
Uses a sufficient range of vocabulary to allow some flexibility and precision; uses less common lexical items with some awareness of style and collocation.
Uses an adequate range of vocabulary for the task; attempts to use less common vocabulary but with some inaccuracy; makes some errors in spelling and word formation.
Uses a limited range of vocabulary; may make noticeable errors in spelling and word formation that may cause some difficulty for the reader.
Uses a wide range of structures with full flexibility and accuracy; rare minor errors occur only as slips.
Uses a wide range of structures; the majority of sentences are error-free; makes only very occasional errors or inappropriacies.
Uses a variety of complex structures; produces frequent error-free sentences; has good control of grammar and punctuation but may make a few errors.
Uses a mix of simple and complex sentence forms; makes some errors in grammar and punctuation but they rarely reduce communication.
Uses only a limited range of structures; attempts complex sentences but these tend to be less accurate than simple sentences.
Content is highly relevant and well-developed; excellent organization with sophisticated transitions; accurate and varied language use; wide range of vocabulary and complex structures.
Content is relevant and complete; logical structure; accurate language with a wide range of vocabulary and structures.
Content is relevant; structure is clear; language is generally accurate with some minor errors; vocabulary is adequate.
Content is basically relevant but incomplete; structure is somewhat loose; language errors are frequent and sometimes impede communication.
Content is only marginally relevant; lacks clear structure; frequent errors seriously impede communication; limited vocabulary.
Outstanding content with depth and insight; sophisticated structure with excellent cohesion; near-native language accuracy; impressive lexical variety and stylistic awareness.
Relevant content with depth; sophisticated structure and cohesion; high level of language accuracy and lexical variety.
Relevant content; clear organization; good control of grammar and vocabulary with some typical errors.
Basically relevant content; structure needs improvement; noticeable errors that occasionally impede understanding; limited vocabulary range.
Limited relevance to the topic; poor organization; frequent errors that seriously impede communication.
Fully addresses the task with insightful content; excellent organization and coherence; highly accurate and sophisticated language; demonstrates mastery of academic writing conventions.
Addresses all parts of the task; well-organized and fluent; high accuracy and wide range of structures; effective use of cohesive devices.
Addresses the task; reasonably organized; language is accurate with occasional errors that do not hinder communication.
Partially addresses the task; organization is unclear in places; frequent errors that sometimes affect understanding.
Minimally addresses the task; poor organization; errors severely impede communication; very limited vocabulary.
Fully satisfies task requirements with excellent format; clear, concise and appropriate language; virtually error-free.
Addresses the task requirements fully; appropriate format and tone; clear and concise language with very few errors.
Addresses main task requirements; generally appropriate format; some minor errors that do not affect communication.
Partially addresses the task; format issues; noticeable errors that may affect clarity.
Fully addresses all requirements; perfect format and tone; clear, fluent and virtually error-free.
Addresses all requirements; appropriate format and tone; accurate language with minimal errors.
Addresses main requirements; generally appropriate format; some errors that do not impede communication.
Partially addresses requirements; format or tone issues; frequent errors affecting clarity.
